title: sesleria heufleriana subsp. hungarica
intro: Sesleria heufleriana subsp. hungarica, also known as the Hungarian moor-grass or Hungarian sesleria, is a subspecies of the Sesleria heufleriana plant, a type of perennial grass native to Europe. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, primarily for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits associated with Sesleria heufleriana subsp. hungarica include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
